cocktails.name: Negroni
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass with ice. Stir until well-chilled, about 20–25 seconds. Strain into a rocks glass over a large ice cube.

cocktails.name: Manhattan
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass with ice. Stir until well-chilled, about 30 seconds. Strain into a chilled coupe or Nick & Nora glass.
cocktails.notes: Bourbon works beautifully here as well.

cocktails.name: Old Fashioned
cocktails.instructions: Combine syrup and bitters in a rocks glass. Add bourbon and a large ice cube. Stir gently for 10–15 seconds to incorporate and chill.
cocktails.notes: Rye whiskey is an equally valid choice here.

cocktails.name: Daiquiri
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients in a shaker with ice. Shake vigorously until well-chilled, about 12–15 seconds. Double-strain into a chilled coupe.
cocktails.notes: One of the most versatile cocktails. Try with different rums for different results — overproof Jamaican changes it entirely.

cocktails.name: Ti' Punch
cocktails.instructions: Cut a disk of lime peel and squeeze the juice into a small rocks glass. Add the cane syrup, then the rhum. Add a small ice cube or serve at room temperature, as is traditional. Stir briefly.
cocktails.notes: Traditionally built to taste — adjust syrup to preference. The lime disk goes in hull and all.

cocktails.name: Paper Plane
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients in a shaker with ice. Shake vigorously until well-chilled. Double-strain into a chilled coupe.
cocktails.garnish: None, or a small lemon twist
cocktails.notes: Equal parts. A perfectly balanced modern classic.
cocktails.creator: Sam Ross (Attaboy)

cocktails.name: Naked and Famous
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients in a shaker with ice. Shake vigorously until well-chilled. Double-strain into a chilled coupe.
cocktails.notes: A Paper Plane riff with mezcal smoke and herbaceous depth from the Chartreuse.

cocktails.name: Last Word
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients in a shaker with ice. Shake vigorously until well-chilled. Double-strain into a chilled coupe.
cocktails.notes: The classic uses Green Chartreuse. Substitute Yellow Chartreuse for a gentler, more citrus-forward version.

cocktails.name: Black Manhattan
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass with ice. Stir until well-chilled, about 30 seconds. Strain into a chilled coupe or Nick & Nora glass.
cocktails.notes: Averna replaces vermouth, giving a richer, more bitter-herbal profile.

cocktails.name: White Negroni
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass with ice. Stir until well-chilled. Strain into a rocks glass over a large ice cube.
cocktails.notes: A lighter, more floral take on the Negroni. The Suze brings bittering gentian without the color.

cocktails.name: Jungle Bird
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients in a shaker with ice. Shake until well-chilled. Strain into a rocks glass over crushed or cracked ice.
cocktails.garnish: Pineapple wedge and leaf, or dried pineapple
cocktails.notes: One of the great tiki drinks. Smith & Cross is a particularly excellent choice here.

cocktails.name: Toronto
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass with ice. Stir until well-chilled. Strain into a chilled coupe.
cocktails.notes: The small amount of Fernet adds menthol complexity without dominating.

cocktails.name: Bamboo
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass with ice. Stir until very well-chilled, about 40 seconds. Strain into a chilled Nick & Nora or coupe.
cocktails.notes: One of the great low-ABV cocktails. The combination of sherry and dry vermouth is remarkably satisfying.

cocktails.name: Adonis
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass with ice. Stir until well-chilled. Strain into a chilled Nick & Nora or coupe.
cocktails.notes: A low-ABV sibling of the Bamboo. Nutty and deeply flavorful.

cocktails.name: Tommy's Margarita
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients in a shaker with ice. Shake vigorously. Strain into a rocks glass over ice. Salt rim optional.
cocktails.notes: Replacing triple sec with agave syrup lets the tequila shine. A cleaner, more agave-forward margarita.

cocktails.name: Margarita
cocktails.instructions: Salt half the rim of a rocks glass if desired. Combine all ingredients in a shaker with ice. Shake vigorously until well-chilled. Strain over fresh ice.
cocktails.notes: Grand Marnier or Dry Curaçao both work well here.

cocktails.name: Oaxacan Old Fashioned
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients in a rocks glass with a large ice cube. Stir gently for 15–20 seconds.
cocktails.notes: The drink that brought mezcal to the cocktail mainstream. The mole bitters echo the agave's earthiness.

cocktails.name: Bijou
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass with ice. Stir until well-chilled. Strain into a chilled Nick & Nora or coupe.
cocktails.notes: Bijou means 'jewel' in French — gin (diamond), chartreuse (emerald), vermouth (ruby). A rich, complex three-way balance.

cocktails.name: Dark & Stormy
cocktails.instructions: Fill a highball glass with ice. Add lime juice. Pour in ginger beer, then float the rum on top by pouring over the back of a spoon.
cocktails.notes: Gosling's Black Seal is the trademarked version. Doorly's 8 or Smith & Cross also work beautifully.

cocktails.name: Rum Old Fashioned
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients in a rocks glass with a large ice cube. Stir gently for 15–20 seconds to chill and slightly dilute.
cocktails.notes: A great way to explore aged rum. Doorly's 8 or Smith & Cross are excellent choices.

cocktails.name: Corpse Reviver No. 2
cocktails.instructions: Rinse a chilled coupe with absinthe and discard the excess. Combine remaining ingredients in a shaker with ice. Shake until well-chilled. Double-strain into the rinsed coupe.
cocktails.notes: Famously noted to 'unrevive the corpse again if taken in excessive quantities.'

cocktails.name: Pisco Sour
cocktails.instructions: Dry shake all ingredients without ice for 15 seconds to emulsify the egg white. Add ice and shake vigorously for another 15 seconds. Double-strain into a chilled coupe.
cocktails.garnish: 3 drops Angostura Bitters on the foam
cocktails.notes: The Peruvian national cocktail. The pisco's floral grape character shines through the frothy texture.

cocktails.name: Cynar Sour
cocktails.instructions: If using egg white, dry shake first without ice for 10 seconds. Add ice and shake vigorously. Double-strain into a chilled coupe.
cocktails.garnish: Orange twist or a few drops of Angostura on the foam
cocktails.notes: The artichoke bitterness of Cynar pairs exceptionally well with rye. A thoughtful, complex sour.

cocktails.name: Trinidad Sour
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients in a shaker with ice. Shake vigorously until well-chilled. Double-strain into a chilled coupe.
cocktails.notes: The cocktail that proved bitters could be a base spirit. The full ounce of Angostura is correct — it becomes velvety and spiced with the orgeat.

cocktails.name: Penicillin
cocktails.instructions: Combine blended Scotch, lemon juice, and honey-ginger syrup in a shaker with ice. Shake until well-chilled. Strain over a large ice cube in a rocks glass. Float the peated Islay Scotch over the top.
cocktails.notes: One of the most important cocktails of the 21st century. Requires Scotch whisky not currently in the inventory.

cocktails.name: The Gilded Veil
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients in a shaker with ice. Shake hard and double strain into a chilled coupe.
cocktails.garnish: Dehydrated or fresh passion fruit wheel, or expressed orange peel discarded
cocktails.notes: Variation: swap Tequila Ocho Plata for Caravedo Pisco for a softer, more floral profile. Add egg white for a silkier texture.
cocktails.creator: Lloid

cocktails.name: Southern Cross
cocktails.instructions: Shake all ingredients except Aperol over pebble or crushed ice. Open pour into a footed pilsner glass. Float Aperol on top.

cocktails.name: Night Nurse (Tropical Negroni)
cocktails.instructions: Add all ingredients to a mixing glass and stir with ice to chill. Strain into a double rocks glass over a large ice cube.
cocktails.notes: Recipe from Imbibe Magazine. Original calls for Punt e Mes; substituted Dolin Rouge. Original calls for aged and overproof Jamaican rum; using Doorly's 8 and Smith & Cross respectively.

cocktails.name: Mai Tai
cocktails.glass: Mai tai or large rocks (16 oz)
cocktails.instructions:
Flash blend ingredients with pebble or crusshed ice.

Open pour into a large rocks, Mai Tai, or snifter (16 oz ideally).

Top with more ice.

Garnish with mint and citrus wheel; float aged overproof rum on top (optional).
cocktails.garnish: Mint sprig, citrus wheel, overproof float

cocktails.name: The Tropic Meridian
cocktails.instructions: Shake Saint James, Chinola, and lime juice with ice. Strain into a Collins glass over fresh ice. Top with soda water.
cocktails.garnish: Lime wheel and passionfruit half if available
cocktails.notes: Simple syrup omitted — Chinola provides sufficient sweetness. If the drink reads too sweet, nudge lime up to 0.875–1 oz. A splash of Nixta Licor de Elote or 2 dashes Angostura bitters are interesting variations.

cocktails.name: Muck & Cacao

ingredients.name: Grand Arôme Rum
ingredients.notes: Canonical version; SBS Origin Le Galion or Holmes Cay Savanna also recommended as variations

ingredients.name: Spiced Molasses Syrup
ingredients.notes: Clove, allspice, cinnamon, nutmeg, star anise; 3:1 sugar to blackstrap molasses ratio

cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass with ice. Stir until well chilled and properly diluted. Strain over a large cube in a rocks glass. Express an orange peel over the surface and discard.
cocktails.notes: Developed with Aficionados Grand Arôme from Le Galion Distillery as the canonical spirit. All three Grand Arôme bottles in inventory are worthy expressions of this recipe, each bringing distinct character. Spiced molasses syrup recipe: 1 cup hot water, 3/4 cup sugar, 1/4 cup blackstrap molasses, 2 cloves, 3 allspice berries, 1 cinnamon stick, pinch nutmeg, 1 star anise — simmer 10 minutes, fine strain, refrigerate up to 3 weeks.
cocktails.creator: Lloid

cocktails.name: Bitter Apple
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients in a shaker with ice. Shake and strain over cracked ice in a rocks glass. Express a lemon peel over the surface and lay it in.
cocktails.notes:
Maple shrub: equal parts maple syrup and apple cider vinegar by volume.

Built around a house-infused Amaro dell'Erborista (cacao nib, kombu, allspice, black cardamom). Bénédictine and the amaro carry residual sweetness.

Variation: swap Bénédictine for 0.25 oz Yellow Chartreuse for a more botanical, grassy profile.

cocktails.name: Metropole
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass and stir for 10-20 seconds. Strain into a cocktail glass and garnish with a cherry.
cocktails.notes: Adapted from Old Waldorf Astoria Bar Days (1931). Structured similarly to a Manhattan but with brandy instead of whiskey and dry vermouth instead of sweet.

cocktails.name: Tango Nuevo
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass and stir for 10-20 seconds. Strain into a rocks glass over one large ice cube. Sprinkle smoked sea salt on top and add lemon twist.
cocktails.notes: Adapted from The One Bottle Cocktail by Maggie Hoffman (2018). Salted caramel flavor profile; Mr Black coffee liqueur works well.

cocktails.name: Sun Valley
cocktails.instructions: Heat the cream to nearly boiling. Turn the heat off and add syrup, egg, and a splash of milk. Whisk together until a nice froth develops. Add rum and serve. Serves 2.
cocktails.notes: Adapted from Esquire's Handbook for Hosts (1949). A mid-century winter warmer. Dark, caramelly rum works best.

cocktails.name: Poet's Dream
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass and stir briefly (10-15 seconds). Strain into a cocktail glass and garnish with lemon peel.
cocktails.notes: From Old Waldorf Astoria Bar Days (1931). A riff on the perfect martini with herbal, moody character.

cocktails.name: Crop Top
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients in a shaker and shake over ice for 30 seconds. Strain into a cocktail glass and garnish with grapefruit peel.
cocktails.notes: A riff on the Paper Plane. Equal-parts format; grapefruit-forward with herbal amaro balance.

cocktails.name: Coco no Coco
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients over ice and shake briefly (about 5 seconds). Strain into a tiki mug or extra tall Collins glass filled with crushed ice, then garnish with spices.
cocktails.notes: Created by Ezra Star of Boston's Drink bar. A creamy tiki drink; demerara rum essential for chocolatey finish.

cocktails.name: Halekulani
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients over ice and shake. Strain into a chilled coupe glass and garnish.
cocktails.garnish: Pineapple wedge, citrus wheel, or pineapple fronds
cocktails.notes: A cross between a classic bourbon sour and a tiki drink. Overproof bourbon recommended to balance the fruity and sweet profile.

cocktails.name: Tour Le Carbet
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients over ice and shake. Double strain into a chilled coupe and garnish with lime wheel.
cocktails.notes: A riff on the classic Royal Bermuda Yacht Club cocktail. Replaces Curaçao with Suze. Named after Le Carbet, a beach commune in Martinique.

cocktails.name: Kir Royale
cocktails.instructions: Add the crème de cassis to a champagne flute or coupe. Top with champagne.
cocktails.notes: Use dry sparkling wine since cassis is already sweet. Adjust cassis amount to taste (0.5-1 oz). Lejay recommended.

cocktails.name: Ghost Coast
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients over ice and shake. Strain into a rocks glass over ice and garnish.
cocktails.notes: An ode to the beaches of northern California. Eucalyptus tincture is house-made from fresh leaves in high-proof spirit. Starts like a margarita then transitions to maple, banana, and herbal eucalyptus.

cocktails.name: Apres-Ski Swizzle
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients in the tiki glass. Top with crushed ice until full. Swizzle with a swizzle stick or barspoon until chilled. Top with more crushed ice and garnish.
cocktails.notes: Adapted from Easy Tiki by Chloe Frechette (2020). Originally created by Nathan Hazard at the Coconut Club in LA. Use green Chartreuse, not yellow.

cocktails.name: Tattletale
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients in a cocktail shaker without ice. Use an immersion blender to blend all ingredients. Add ice and shake vigorously for ten seconds. Strain into a cocktail glass.
cocktails.notes: A funky yet simple drink. Marshmallow cream creates egg-like thickness. Pear or pumpkin butter can substitute for apple butter.

cocktails.name: Yuzu Choco
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass and stir for approximately 10 seconds. Strain into a rocks glass over one large cube and garnish with lemon peel.
cocktails.notes: Adapted from The Japanese Art of the Cocktail by Masahiro Urushido and Michael Anstendig (2021). Notably dry with aromas of sweet grains and lemon.

cocktails.name: Gentleman's Exchange
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass. Stir for approximately ten seconds. Pour into a rocks glass over a large cube. Garnish with grapefruit peel.
cocktails.notes: Also known as Tuxedo No. 2. Adapted from Leo Robitschek's The NoMad Cocktail Book (2019). A strong rocks cocktail with coffee and amaro emphasis.

cocktails.name: Shafer's Eureka
cocktails.instructions: Brew chamomile tea and allow it to cool. Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass and stir briefly until the glass begins to frost. Strain into a cocktail glass and optionally garnish.
cocktails.notes: Adapted from Vermouth by Adam Ford (2015). A delicate session cocktail highlighting dry vermouth. Features a pretty yellow color with grape and chamomile notes.

cocktails.name: Electron
cocktails.instructions: Combine pink peppercorn and salt in a spice grinder and process until fine. Rim cocktail glass with lime juice followed by peppercorn mixture. Combine remaining ingredients with ice and shake for 15 seconds. Strain into the prepared glass.
cocktails.notes: A funky brunch drink pairing carrot juice with mezcal. Use mezcal with salty, savory profile.

cocktails.name: Apium
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients except salt in a shaker. Shake for 30 seconds. Strain into ice-filled glass. Garnish with a pinch of salt and optionally a lime wheel.
cocktails.notes: A smoky and vegetal riff on the margarita. Celery bitters add savory depth. Also listed as Opium on the site.

cocktails.name: Paris is Burning
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients over ice in a cocktail shaker and shake. Strain into a chilled cocktail glass, served up.
cocktails.notes: Adapted from The NoMad Cocktail Book by Leo Robitschek (2019). Split base of mezcal and gin. Fruity, herbal, and smoky bouquet.

cocktails.name: Royal Bermuda Yacht Club
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients with ice and shake. Double strain into a chilled cocktail glass and garnish with lime wheel.
cocktails.notes: 1950s classic attributed to Trader Vic. Blends tiki canon with classic daiquiri style. Aged rum works best.

cocktails.name: Paloma
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients except soda in a highball or Collins glass over ice and stir. Optionally coat the rim with lime juice and roll in freshly ground salt. Top with soda and garnish.
cocktails.notes: Uses fresh juices rather than grapefruit soda. Dry, slightly bitter, and absolutely refreshing.

cocktails.name: French Canadian
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients over ice and stir until well chilled. Strain into a chilled coupe glass, express lemon oils over the top, then use peel as garnish.
cocktails.notes: Use quality Cognac and real maple syrup. Champagne vinegar provides complex acidity. Warm vanilla and nutty cognac with maple sweetness.

cocktails.name: Vesper

ingredients.name: Kina Lillet
ingredients.notes: Tempus Fugit Kina L'Aero d'Or recommended

cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass and stir until the glass becomes frosty. Strain into a cocktail glass and garnish with lemon twist.
cocktails.notes: From Ian Fleming's Casino Royale (1953). This version removes vodka and uses more reasonable proportions. Stirred, not shaken.

cocktails.name: Last Man Standing
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass and stir for ten seconds. Express oils from both orange twists onto the mixing glass. Strain contents into cocktail glass and garnish with one orange twist.
cocktails.notes: A bold fernet-forward Negroni variation. Equal-parts format. Fernet Vallet recommended over Fernet-Branca. A bartender's drink.

cocktails.name: Groceries for the Queen
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients with a few pebbles of ice and shake. Pour into a glass filled with pebble ice and top with more pebble ice. Garnish with cherries or orange peel.
cocktails.notes: Adapted from Sother Teague's I'm Just Here for the Drinks (2018). Fruity and balanced modern drink featuring amaro Montenegro.

cocktails.name: Death in the Afternoon
cocktails.instructions: Add absinthe to a champagne glass. Top with very cold champagne.
cocktails.notes: Attributed to Ernest Hemingway, published in a 1935 cocktail anthology. Absinthe will louche as champagne is added. Adjust absinthe to taste (0.25-1.5 oz).

cocktails.name: Erin
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass and stir for ten seconds. Strain into a rocks glass over one large cube and garnish with both peels.
cocktails.notes: Created by Toby Cecchini at Long Island Bar in Brooklyn. Structured similarly to a Boulevardier but sweeter and fruitier.

cocktails.name: Buttermilk Flip
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients in a shaker without ice and dry shake until emulsified, approximately 20 seconds. Fill shaker with ice and shake until chilled and frothy. Strain into a rocks glass over ice and garnish with grated hazelnut.
cocktails.notes: Adapted from The One Bottle Cocktail by Maggie Hoffman (2018). Buttermilk provides lighter body and tartness. A decadent alternative to holiday classics.

cocktails.name: Flannel Shirt
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients in a shaker and shake. Strain into a rocks glass over cracked ice.
cocktails.notes: Fruity and spiced with velvety texture. Apple, citrus, and malt notes; clove and herbal finish. Pig's Nose and Monkey Shoulder recommended scotch options.

cocktails.name: El Tigre
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients except soda water in a cocktail shaker and dry shake for 5 seconds. Fill shaker with ice and shake again for 10-15 seconds. Fill a small Collins or rocks glass with soda water, then strain remaining contents on top.
cocktails.notes: A spin on the Ramos Gin Fizz at the intersection of a flip and a fizz. Tequila and aquavit split base. Linie Aquavit and Cimarron reposado recommended.

cocktails.name: Pale End of the Day
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass and stir until the sides are frosty. Serve in a chilled cocktail glass and garnish with pear slice.
cocktails.notes: Quality blanc vermouth essential (Del Professore Classic Bianco recommended). St. George's spiced pear liqueur works beautifully.

cocktails.name: Star
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass and stir for ten seconds. Strain into a cocktail glass and garnish with lemon twist.
cocktails.notes: From George Kappeler's Modern American Drinks (1895). A Manhattan riff with apple brandy yielding autumn flavors.

cocktails.name: Punch a la Romaine
cocktails.instructions: Add all ingredients except champagne to a shaker and dry shake without ice. Add ice and shake again. Strain into a cocktail glass filled with shaved ice. Top with champagne.
cocktails.notes: A fanciful cooler bridging kitchen and bar. Dry shake first to maximize egg white volume. Blood orange can be used for a pink hue.

cocktails.name: Odd Bedfellows
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients with a few pebbles of ice and shake. Pour into a glass filled with pebble ice, and top with more pebble ice. Garnish with pineapple wedge, mint, and/or grated bitter chocolate.
cocktails.garnish: Pineapple wedge, mint sprig, and/or grated bitter chocolate
cocktails.notes: From Sother Teague's I'm Just Here for the Drinks (2018). Decadent tiki drink with bracing fernet complexity.

cocktails.name: Lavender Lady
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients except champagne in a mixing glass and stir for twenty seconds. Strain into a cocktail glass. Top with champagne and garnish with lavender buds.
cocktails.notes: A brisk blast of spring herbals. Lemon-forward gins work best. Use neutral sparkling wine with good acidity.

cocktails.name: Spanish Coffee
cocktails.instructions: Create a slit in a lime wedge and coat the top lip of the glass generously. Dip the rim into sugar to form a band. Add rum and ignite it; use the flame to caramelize the sugar on the rim. Pour in liqueurs and coffee, top with whipped cream and a dusting of nutmeg.
cocktails.notes: A Portland original with dazzling tableside preparation. Uses navy-strength rum instead of 151-proof for safety while maintaining caramel flavor. Fresh whipped cream preferred.

cocktails.name: Fionia
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients over ice and stir. Strain into a coupe and garnish with chamomile flowers.
cocktails.notes: Adapted from Selma Slabiak's Spirit of the North (2018). Scandinavian-inspired with approachable complexity. Dry amontillado sherry recommended.

cocktails.name: Doctor Cocktail
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients over ice and shake. Strain into a cocktail glass and garnish.
cocktails.notes: From Ted Haigh's Vintage Spirits and Forgotten Cocktails (2004). A funky, short, complex proto-tiki classic. Dates back to 1917.

cocktails.name: Harvard
cocktails.instructions: Combine cognac, vermouth, and bitters over ice and stir until chilled. Strain into a chilled cocktail glass. Top with soda water and garnish.
cocktails.notes: From George J. Kappeler's Modern American Drinks (1895). A brandy Manhattan variation with soda water for a lighter profile.

cocktails.name: Suburban
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients over ice and stir. Strain into a chilled cocktail glass, served up.
cocktails.notes: A winter cocktail with vanilla, cherry, and orange flavors. Similar to a Manhattan but smoother with port and rum additions.

cocktails.name: Chicago Cocktail
cocktails.instructions: Optionally sugar the rim of a cocktail glass. Combine brandy, orange liqueur, and bitters over ice and stir. Strain into glass and top with champagne.
cocktails.notes: A spirit-forward sparkling drink with high brandy ratio. Precise proportions crucial. China-China Amer can substitute for orange liqueur.

cocktails.name: Pære Dansk
cocktails.instructions: Add all ingredients except champagne over ice and shake. Pour over cubes in a tumbler and top with sparkling wine. Garnish with pear.
cocktails.notes: Created by Selma Slabiak, head bartender at NYC's Nordic restaurant Aska. St. George's Spiced Pear Liqueur recommended. Falernum can substitute orgeat.

cocktails.name: La Dominicana
cocktails.instructions: Whip together heavy cream using a whisk or stand mixer. Add rum and coffee liqueur to a mixing glass and stir over ice. Strain into a cocktail glass and float whipped cream on top.
cocktails.notes: Created by Sasha Petraske around 2011. An exceptional upgrade to the White Russian. Smooth aged rum avoids harshness with coffee liqueur.

cocktails.name: Cynar Flip
cocktails.instructions: Dry shake the whole egg in a cocktail shaker for 20 seconds. Add Cynar, simple syrup, and ice before shaking again for another 20 seconds. Strain into a cocktail glass and top with several dashes of bitters in the foam.
cocktails.notes: Dry shake vigorously twice for best frothy head. Works well with fernet, Zucca, amaro Ciociaro, or Campari substitutions.

cocktails.name: Diki-Diki
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients over ice and shake. Strain into a chilled cocktail glass, served up.
cocktails.notes: From Vintage Spirits and Forgotten Cocktails by Ted Haigh. Slightly modified from Robert Vermiere's 1922 original. Reminiscent of a more complex Jack Rose.

cocktails.name: Grasshopper
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients over ice and shake for 30 seconds. Strain into a chilled cocktail glass and garnish with mint.
cocktails.notes: Quality crème de menthe is essential. No ice cream or green food coloring. A balanced, creamy dessert drink.

cocktails.name: Monkey Gland
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients over ice and shake. Strain into a chilled coupe and garnish with orange wedge.
cocktails.notes: A simple mix of gin, orange, grenadine, and absinthe with beautiful candy-orange color. Fresh orange juice essential.

cocktails.name: Suissesse
cocktails.instructions: Combine all ingredients over ice and shake vigorously. Strain into a cocktail glass and garnish with grated nutmeg. Express lemon oils and discard peel.
cocktails.notes: The culmination of absinthe in the mixed drink format. Dead Rabbit adaptation featuring crème de cacao and aquavit. Serve immediately.

cocktails.name: Alpine Odyssey
cocktails.instructions: Wet shake or flash blend all ingredients over small ice. Open pour into a sling glass and top with pebble ice.
cocktails.notes: Suggested: Dakabend Oaxacan Pot Still Rum, Paranubes Oaxacan Aguardiente de Caña, Dolin Génépy le Chamois, Hendrick's Gin.

cocktails.name: Bad Banana
cocktails.instructions: Wet shake all ingredients and strain into an oversized double rocks or Mai Tai glass. Top with pebble ice, add banana leaf and straw, then pack with pebble ice to form a small dome. Grate nutmeg on top.
cocktails.notes: High proof, big banana. Suggested: Tempus Fugit Crème de Banane, Old Overholt BIB Rye, Worthy Park Overproof.

cocktails.name: Bird Song
cocktails.instructions: Stir all ingredients and strain into a double rocks glass over a large cube. Place jalapeño slice on top of cube.
cocktails.creator: Ian

cocktails.name: Box of Rain
cocktails.instructions: Wet shake all ingredients and strain into a chilled Nick & Nora glass. Float mint leaf on top.
cocktails.creator: Ian

cocktails.name: Brancolada

ingredients.name: Coconut Syrup
ingredients.notes: blend equal masses coconut milk and cane syrup

cocktails.instructions: Flash blend all ingredients over small ice and open pour into a collins glass. Fill with pebble ice and garnish with mint and orange wheel.
cocktails.notes: A Fernet-ified piña colada. Coconut syrup: blend equal masses coconut milk and cane syrup. Suggested: Fernet Branca, Smith & Cross, Hampden HLCF Classic.

cocktails.name: Cane Reviver #2
cocktails.instructions: Spritz a chilled Nick & Nora glass with absinthe. Stir remaining ingredients over ice and strain into the glass.
cocktails.notes: A tropical adaptation of a classic (Corpse Reviver #2). Suggested: Rhum Clément Créole Shrubb, Père Labat 59° Rhum Agricole.
cocktails.creator: Ian

cocktails.name: Caribou Lou (Remix)
cocktails.instructions: Flash blend all ingredients (except float) over small ice and open pour into a hurricane glass. Top with pebble ice and garnish with the banana leaf. Float the remaining 151 on top.
cocktails.garnish: Banana leaf, 0.5 oz aged 151 rum float
cocktails.notes: Suggested: Maggie's Farm Falernum, Havana Club 3 Años, Hamilton Demerara 151.

cocktails.name: The Cask of Pinallado
cocktails.instructions: Wet shake all ingredients (except 151) over small ice and open pour into a hurricane glass. Fill with pebble ice and float the 151. Grate nutmeg on top.
cocktails.garnish: 0.5 oz aged 151 rum float, freshly grated nutmeg
cocktails.notes: Another pineapple-Amontillado cocktail. Suggested: Chairman's Reserve Saint Lucia Rum, Pineapple-infused Doctor Bird Rum, Hamilton Demerara 151.
cocktails.creator: Ian

cocktails.name: China Cat Sunflower
cocktails.instructions: Wet shake all ingredients over small ice and open pour into a Mai Tai or double rocks glass. Garnish with pineapple wheel and huacatay.
cocktails.notes: A Pan-American Mai Tai. Suggested: Sotol Por Siempre, Paranubes Añejo Oaxacan Aguardiente de Caña.
cocktails.creator: Ian

cocktails.name: Cynapple
cocktails.instructions: Lightly coat a pineapple wedge with sugar and blowtorch until caramelized. While pineapple cools, wet shake all ingredients and strain into a chilled coupe or Nick & Nora. Garnish with the pineapple wedge.
cocktails.notes: The spice of rye meets pineapple umami. Suggested: Hamilton Demerara 151, Old Overholt BIB Rye, Rittenhouse BIB Rye.
cocktails.creator: Ian

cocktails.name: The Derelict
cocktails.instructions: Flash blend all ingredients over small ice and open pour into tiki mug. Fill with pebble ice and garnish with the mint sprig. Place the lime half on top and fill with 151. Light the 151 and express some citrus twists over the flame.
cocktails.garnish: Aged 151 rum (in lime half, flamed), scored and hollowed lime half, citrus twists, mint sprig
cocktails.notes: The kitchen sink and then some. Banana blend: blend equal parts by mass banana liqueur with overripe banana fruit and strain through a nut milk bag. Suggested: Père Labat 59° Rhum Agricole, Appleton 8yr, Hamilton Demerara 86 & 151, Buffalo Trace.

cocktails.name: Dark Star
cocktails.instructions: Wet shake all ingredients and strain into a chilled Nick & Nora glass. Garnish with the cherry.
cocktails.notes: A cannabis-infused rum-based Aviation. Cannabis is faster acting than a standard edible but shorter lasting. See book for cannabis gin infusion recipe using iSi canister technique.

cocktails.name: Dire Wolf
cocktails.instructions: Spritz a chilled Nick & Nora glass with absinthe. Stir remaining ingredients over ice and strain into the glass.
cocktails.notes: A Zombie served up. Suggested: Hamilton Demerara 86, Worthy Park Overproof, Hamilton Demerara 151.

cocktails.name: Disgruntled Mai Tai
cocktails.instructions: Wet shake all ingredients and open pour into a Mai Tai or double rocks glass. Fill with pebble ice. Garnish with the mint and lime wheel. Dash some Underberg on top and stick it into the ice to garnish.
cocktails.notes: A bitter Mai Tai. Suggested: Pierre Ferrand Dry Curaçao, Smith & Cross Traditional Jamaican Rum.

cocktails.name: Do Pineapples Dream of Tropical Drinks?
cocktails.instructions: Stir all ingredients over ice and strain into a rocks glass with a large cube.
cocktails.notes: A rich, umami-forward stirred drink. Suggested: Pineapple-infused Doctor Bird Rum.

cocktails.name: El Diablo
cocktails.instructions: Wet shake all ingredients and open pour into a collins glass filled with cracked ice. Top with ginger beer and gently stir to incorporate. Garnish with the lime wheel.
cocktails.notes: A fruity tequila cooler. Suggested: Combier Liqueur de Cassis, Arette Reposado Tequila.

cocktails.name: Elusive Dreams
cocktails.instructions: Wet shake all ingredients and strain into a chilled Nick & Nora glass. Garnish with the pineapple wedge.
cocktails.notes: A riff removed from a Hotel Nacional. Suggested: Tempus Fugit Crème de Banane, Havana Club 3 Años, Pineapple-infused Doctor Bird Rum.

cocktails.name: Existential Crisis #2
cocktails.instructions: Stir all ingredients over ice and strain into a chilled Nick & Nora glass. Express grapefruit twist and garnish.
cocktails.notes: What is a modern tropical drink? Suggested: Pineapple-infused Doctor Bird Rum.

cocktails.name: Ferpleberry
cocktails.instructions: Wet shake all ingredients and strain into a chilled Nick & Nora glass. Garnish with the pineapple wedge and strawberry slice on a cocktail pick.
cocktails.notes: Amontillado pairs with strawberry-pineapple. Suggested: Maggie's Farm Falernum, Fernet Branca, Worthy Park 109.

cocktails.name: Fire on the Mountain
cocktails.instructions: Stir all ingredients over ice and strain into a double rocks glass over a large cube. Express orange twist and garnish.
cocktails.notes: A Rum Fire-inspired Negroni riff. Suggested: Carpano Antica, Cocchi Vermouth di Torino.

cocktails.name: Foolish Heart
cocktails.instructions: Gently muddle two basil leaves in a shaker with the syrups. Add ice and remaining ingredients and shake. Strain into a collins glass and fill with pebble ice.
cocktails.notes: An herbal, spiced Port au Prince adaptation. Suggested: Neisson Élevé Sous Bois Rhum, Clairin Le Rocher, Clairin Sonson.
cocktails.creator: Ian

cocktails.name: Friend of the Devil
cocktails.instructions: Wet shake all ingredients and strain into a chilled coupe. Cut the tops of the chiles and make a slit into the top of each. Stick them onto the rim of the coupe à la devil's horns.
cocktails.notes: A mango bourbon sour. Suggested: Maggie's Farm Falernum, Michter's US*1 Kentucky Straight Bourbon.

cocktails.name: Get It Done
cocktails.instructions: Pour beer into a large pitcher filled with ice. Shake the remaining ingredients over ice and then open pour into the pitcher. Gently stir to incorporate. Pour into shatter-resistant glasses.
cocktails.notes: A spicy, fruity michelada. Batched drink — serves a group. Suggested: Modelo Especial.

cocktails.name: Killer Bird
cocktails.instructions: Mix the ingredients of each drink in separate shakers with ice. Fill two hurricane glasses with pebble ice. Shake the Painkiller and strain half into each glass. Shake the Jungle Bird and gently layer it on top of each Painkiller. Torch cinnamon sticks until smoldering, extinguish, and garnish.
cocktails.garnish: Two dehydrated orange wheels, two smoldering cinnamon sticks, grated nutmeg
cocktails.notes: A Jungle Bird × Painkiller mashup. Serves two — make both drinks simultaneously and layer in two glasses. Suggested: Hamilton Demerara 151, Smith & Cross, Doorly's 12yr.

cocktails.name: Mammoth Tusk
cocktails.instructions: Flash blend all ingredients over a few small ice cubes. Open pour into a tiki mug and fill with pebble ice. Garnish with mint and dash some Peychaud's on top.
cocktails.garnish: Peychaud's bitters (dashed on top), mint sprig
cocktails.notes: A Mai Tai meets a Chartreuse Swizzle. Suggested: Maggie's Farm Falernum, Père Labat 59° Rhum Agricole.

cocktails.name: Margot Tenenbaum
cocktails.instructions: Wet shake all ingredients and strain into a chilled Nick & Nora. Express lemon twist and discard. No garnish.
cocktails.notes: An herbal rye sour. Suggested: Cappelletti Amaro Sfumato Rabarbaro, Michter's US*1 Single Barrel Rye, Wild Turkey 101 Rye.

cocktails.name: Morning Dew
cocktails.instructions: Wet shake all ingredients and strain into a chilled Nick & Nora glass.
cocktails.notes: A juicy, grassy daiquiri variation. Suggested: Paranubes Oaxacan Aguardiente de Caña, Père Labat 59° Rhum Agricole.
cocktails.creator: Ian

cocktails.name: New-England Currant
cocktails.instructions: Wet shake all ingredients and open pour into a double rocks glass. Fill with cracked ice. Garnish with the fresh currants, or whatever you feel like because you don't have fresh currants.
cocktails.garnish: Strand of fresh red currants (or improvise)
cocktails.notes: A currant whiskey sour. Suggested: Cappelletti Amaro Sfumato Rabarbaro, Michter's US*1 Sour Mash.

cocktails.name: Panic Arrack
cocktails.instructions: Wet shake all ingredients and open pour into a double rocks glass. Fill with cracked ice. Garnish with the dehydrated lemon wheel.
cocktails.notes: A sour with malt, red fruit, and baking spice notes. Suggested: Old Forester 1870 Original Batch.

cocktails.name: Petty Crime
cocktails.instructions: Wet shake all ingredients and open pour into a Mai Tai or double rocks glass. Top with pebble ice and garnish with mint.
cocktails.notes: A tropical concoction of banana and fernet. Suggested: Tempus Fugit Crème de Banane, Fernet Branca, Smith & Cross.

cocktails.name: PX Sour
cocktails.instructions: Wet shake all ingredients and strain into a chilled coupe. Float dehydrated lemon wheel on top.
cocktails.notes: A rye sour with sherry and apple. Suggested: Laird's Straight Apple Brandy, Michter's US*1 Single Barrel Straight Rye.

cocktails.name: Queen Jane Approximately
cocktails.instructions: Rinse a chilled Nick & Nora glass with Green Chartreuse and discard. Use grapefruit twist to wet the outside rim and rim with toasted fennel salt. Muddle grapes in a shaker, add ice and remaining ingredients, and shake. Strain into the glass.
cocktails.garnish: Grapefruit twist, toasted fennel salt rim
cocktails.notes: A rum adaptation of Gypsy Wedding. Suggested: Maggie's Farm Falernum, Linie Aquavit, Père Labat 59° Rhum Agricole.

cocktails.name: Ranglum
cocktails.instructions: Stir all ingredients over ice and strain into a double rocks glass over a large cube. Use a zester to score a lime and cut a particularly nice looking wedge to garnish.
cocktails.notes: A funky falernum sour. Suggested: Maggie's Farm Falernum, Smith & Cross.

cocktails.name: Ridge Racer
cocktails.instructions: Wet shake all ingredients and strain into a chilled coupe. Float dehydrated lime wheel on top.
cocktails.notes: Funky, herbal, expensive — worth it. Served-down version: 1 barspoon each crème de cacao and lime punch syrup; 1-2 dashes allspice dram.

cocktails.name: Shipwrecked
cocktails.instructions: Wet shake all ingredients over small ice and open pour into a tiki mug. Top with pebble ice and garnish with mint and lime wheel.
cocktails.notes: Pineapple, rhubarb, and rum. Suggested: Cappelletti Amaro Sfumato Rabarbaro, Smith & Cross, Hamilton Demerara 86.

cocktails.name: The Smoke Also Rises
cocktails.instructions: Wet shake all ingredients and strain into a chilled coupe. Garnish with the lime wheel.
cocktails.notes: A rhubarb-ed Hemingway daiquiri. Suggested: Cappelletti Amaro Sfumato Rabarbaro, Havana Club 3 Años.

cocktails.name: Tarzan
cocktails.instructions: Wet shake all ingredients and open pour into a collins glass. Fill with ice and top with soda. Garnish with the orange wheel.
cocktails.notes: A long drink with gin, Campari, and pineapple. Suggested: Hendrick's Gin.

cocktails.name: Touch of Grey
cocktails.instructions: Wet shake all ingredients and open pour into a double rocks glass. Fill with pebble ice and garnish with the mint.
cocktails.notes: A Midnight Stinger with Jamaican rum. Suggested: Fernet Branca, Smith & Cross.

cocktails.name: Tropical Fluff
cocktails.instructions: Sprinkle sugar on an orange wedge and blowtorch until caramelized. Flash blend all ingredients with a few small ice cubes until you can no longer hear them. Open pour into a small collins glass and garnish with the caramelized orange wedge.
cocktails.notes: A funky Garibaldi variation. Suggested: Tempus Fugit Crème de Banane, Smith & Cross.

cocktails.name: Tuscan Toucan
cocktails.instructions: Flash blend all ingredients (except 151) over a few small ice and open pour into a sling glass. Fill with pebble ice and garnish with the mint and citrus wheel. Float 151 on top.
cocktails.garnish: Mint sprig, dehydrated citrus wheel, 0.75 oz aged 151 rum float
cocktails.notes: Fruity, funky, bittersweet. Suggested: Wray & Nephew White Overproof, Amaro Montenegro, Hamilton Demerara 151.

cocktails.name: Vernon's Cloak
cocktails.instructions: Wet shake all ingredients over small ice and open pour into a double rocks glass. Fill with pebble ice and garnish with the mint.
cocktails.notes: Baking spices and honey with Cynar and rum. Suggested: Appleton Estate 8 Year Reserve.

cocktails.name: White Zombie
cocktails.instructions: Flash blend all ingredients over a few small ice and open pour into a tiki mug. Fill with pebble ice and garnish with the mint and citrus wheel.
cocktails.garnish: Mint sprig, citrus wheel, brandied cherry
cocktails.notes: Half your bar in a glass. Suggested: Maggie's Farm Falernum, Wray & Nephew White Overproof, Hamilton Demerara 86 & 151, The Real McCoy 5yr.

cocktails.name: 8th Wonder
cocktails.instructions: In a cocktail tin combine all the ingredients except the tonic water. Fill a highball glass with 1¼ inch ice cubes. Prime the highball glass with the tonic water. Cover the cocktail tin and shake. Strain the cocktail into the highball glass.

cocktails.name: Angelo and Rocco
cocktails.instructions: Combine all the ingredients in a cocktail tin. Fill the tin with ice cover and shake vigorously. Strain the cocktail into a coupe.

cocktails.name: Anthora
cocktails.instructions: In a cocktail tin combine all the ingredients except the garnishes. Fill a cobbler glass with crushed ice. Add three 1¼ inch ice cubes to the cocktail tin. Cover and whip. Strain the cocktail into the cobbler glass and top with additional crushed ice. Garnish with the mint plouche and grapefruit twist.

cocktails.name: Banana Stand
cocktails.instructions: In a cocktail tin combine all the ingredients except the garnish. Fill a Pilsner glass with crushed ice. Add three 1¼ inch ice cubes to the cocktail tin. Cover and whip. Strain the cocktail into the Pilsner glass and top with additional crushed ice. Shave bittersweet chocolate over the top of the cocktail for garnish.

cocktails.name: Broken Spanish
cocktails.instructions: In a chilled mixing glass combine all the ingredients except the Becherovka and garnish. Fill the mixing glass with 1¼ inch ice cubes and stir. Using an atomizer rinse a chilled coupe with six sprays of Becherovka. Strain the cocktail into the coupe. Express the grapefruit twist over the drink and then drop it into the glass.

cocktails.name: Bullingdon Club Cup

ingredients.name: Cold Fever Tree Tonic Water Plus More

cocktails.instructions: Use the lemon wedge to moisten the top inch of the outer rim of a chilled highball glass. Gently roll the highball glass in salt to rim half of the glass. Shake off excess salt. Use the corner of a folded cocktail napkin to make the salt a consistent thickness. Fill the highball glass with 1¼ inch ice cubes. Add all the remaining ingredients except the tonic water to a cocktail tin. Add three 1¼ inch ice cubes to the tin. Cover and whip vigorously. Add the tonic water to the tin. Strain the cocktail into the highball glass using a hawthorne strainer. Fill the glass with additional tonic water.

cocktails.name: Cardinale
cocktails.instructions: In a cocktail tin muddle a cucumber slice in the ginger syrup then add the remaining ingredients except the club soda and garnish. Fill a highball glass with crushed ice. Add three 1¼ inch cubes to the tin. Cover and whip. Strain the cocktail into the highball glass. Add the club soda and garnish with the remaining cucumber slice.

cocktails.name: Clip Joint Cup
cocktails.garnish: grapefruit twists 1 for muddling and 1, cucumber slices 1 for muddling and 1, Mint plouche
cocktails.instructions: In a cocktail tin muddle a grapefruit twist and a cucumber slice with the demerara syrup. Fill a highball glass with 1¼ inch ice cubes and prime it with the club soda and tonic water. Add the rest of the ingredients except the garnishes to the tin. Cover and shake. Strain the cocktail into the highball glass. Garnish with the mint plouche and remaining cucumber slice and grapefruit twist.

cocktails.name: Common Thread
cocktails.instructions: Use the lemon wedge to moisten the top inch of the outer rim of a chilled highball glass. Gently roll the highball glass in chile salt to rim half of the glass. Shake off excess salt. Use the corner of a folded cocktail napkin to make the salt a consistent thickness. Fill the highball glass with 1¼ inch ice cubes. Add the remaining ingredients except the club soda and garnish to a cocktail tin. Add three 1¼ inch ice cubes to the tin. Cover and shake vigorously. Add 1 ounce of the club soda to the cocktail tin. Strain the cocktail into the highball glass using a hawthorne strainer. Fill the glass with the remaining ¾ ounce club soda and garnish with the cucumber slice around the inside rim of the glass.

cocktails.name: Cortez
cocktails.instructions: Combine all the ingredients except the Champagne in a cocktail tin. Fill a highball glass with 1¼ inch ice cubes and add the Champagne. Fill the cocktail tin with ice. Cover and shake vigorously. Strain the cocktail into the highball glass.

cocktails.name: Cup of Joe
cocktails.instructions: In a cocktail tin combine all the ingredients except the tonic water and grapefruit twist. Fill a highball glass with 1¼ inch ice cubes. Prime the highball glass with the tonic water. Cover the cocktail tin and shake. Strain the cocktail into the highball glass. Express the oils of the grapefruit twist over the drink then discard the twist.

cocktails.name: Danger Haus
cocktails.instructions: Combine all the ingredients except the garnish in a cocktail tin. Wrap the cucumber slice around the inside of the highball glass starting from the bottom and fill the glass with crushed ice. Add three 1¾ inch ice cubes to the cocktail tin. Cover and whip. Strain the cocktail into the highball glass.
cocktails.notes: Creator: Jane Danger and Pietro Collina

cocktails.name: Folklore

ingredients.name: Folksbier Sif Dry Hopped Table Beer

cocktails.instructions: In a cocktail tin combine all the ingredients except the beer. Fill a highball glass with 1¼ inch ice cubes and add the beer. Fill the tin with ice. Cover and shake vigorously. Strain the cocktail into the highball glass.
cocktails.notes: Creator: Pietro Collina and Julia Reingold

cocktails.name: Fresa y Cervesa
cocktails.instructions: In a cocktail tin muddle a cucumber slice and combine the remaining ingredients except the beer and garnish. Wrap the cucumber garnish around the inside of a highball glass starting from the bottom. Fill the glass with 1¼ inch ice cubes and add the beer. Add ice to the tin. Cover and shake vigorously. Strain the cocktail into the highball glass.

cocktails.name: Haymarket
cocktails.instructions: In a cocktail tin muddle a cucumber slice and combine the remaining ingredients except the beer and the garnish. Wrap the whole length cucumber garnish around the inside of a highball glass starting from the bottom. Fill the glass with 1¼ inch ice cubes and add the beer. Fill the tin with ice. Cover and shake vigorously. Strain the cocktail into the highball glass and garnish with the remaining cucumber slice.

cocktails.name: Hot Broth
cocktails.instructions: Fill a hot cocktail glass with hot water in order to heat it. In a small saucepan combine all the ingredients and warm over medium heat until the cocktail comes to a simmer. Discard the hot water in the cocktail glass then pour in the cocktail.

cocktails.name: Howie in the Jungle
cocktails.instructions: In a cocktail tin combine all the ingredients except the club soda and garnishes. Fill a highball glass with 1¼ inch ice cubes. Fill the cocktail tin with 1¼ inch ice cubes. Cover and shake vigorously. Add the club soda to the cocktail tin. Strain the cocktail into the highball glass. Skewer the orange half wheel through the ends place the mint sprigs through the center and garnish the cocktail.

cocktails.name: Into the Woods
cocktails.instructions: In a cocktail tin combine all the ingredients except the garnish. Seal the tin and dry shake to emulsify the egg white into the cocktail. Carefully open the tin and fill with 1¼ inch ice cubes. Seal it again and shake vigorously. Strain the cocktail into a chilled coupe using a hawthorne strainer and a fine tea strainer. Garnish with the sprig of rosemary.

cocktails.name: Jane Stanley
cocktails.garnish: orange half wheels 1 for muddling and 2
cocktails.instructions: In a cocktail tin muddle an orange half wheel then add the remaining ingredients except the garnish. Fill a cobbler glass with crushed ice. Add three 1¼ inch ice cubes to the cocktail tin. Cover and whip. Strain the cocktail into the cobbler glass and garnish with the remaining orange half wheels.

cocktails.name: Joe Danger
cocktails.instructions: Combine all the ingredients except the garnishes in a cocktail tin. Fill a Pilsner glass with crushed ice. Add three 1¼ inch ice cubes to the cocktail tin. Cover and whip. Strain the cocktail into the Pilsner glass and top with additional crushed ice. Express the oils of the grapefruit twist over the top of the drink. Garnish with the mint plouche and the expressed grapefruit twist.

cocktails.name: Ma Cherie
cocktails.instructions: In a cocktail tin muddle the celery then combine all the remaining ingredients except the garnish. Wrap the cucumber garnish around the inside of a highball glass starting from the bottom. Fill the highball glass with crushed ice. Add three 1¼ inch ice cubes to the cocktail tin. Cover and whip. Strain the cocktail into the highball glass.

cocktails.name: Midnight Bramble
cocktails.instructions: Combine all the ingredients except the blackberries and crÏme de mÜre in a cocktail tin. Place the blackberries on the bottom of a double rocks glass and fill the glass with crushed ice. Add three 1¼ inch cubes to the cocktail tin. Cover and whip. Strain the cocktail into the double rocks glass. Fill the glass with more crushed ice and make a mound of crushed ice up over the rim of the glass. Pour the crÏme de mÜre over the top of the dome.

cocktails.name: Monferrato
cocktails.instructions: Combine all the ingredients except the Champagne and orange twist in a mixing glass. Fill the mixing glass with ice and stir. Strain the cocktail into a Champagne flute and top with Champagne. Express the oils of the orange twist over the glass then rub the twist on the rim of the glass and discard the twist.

cocktails.name: On the Vine
cocktails.instructions: Using an iSi canister see this page carbonate the saline solution cane syrup amontillado sherry tomato water and fino sherry. Place a 2 inch ice cube in a double rocks glass. Add the vinegar lemon juice and 4 ounces of the carbonated liquid to the rocks glass and stir gently for 5 seconds. Garnish with the sprig of lemon thyme.

cocktails.name: Seoul Mate
cocktails.instructions: In a cocktail tin combine all the ingredients except the garnish. Fill a highball glass with crushed ice. Add three 1¼ inch ice cubes to the cocktail tin. Cover and whip. Strain the cocktail into the highball glass and top with additional crushed ice. Garnish with the lemon wheel.
cocktails.notes: Creator: Leo Robitschek and Pietro Collina

cocktails.name: Shiitake Dirty to Me
cocktails.instructions: In a double rocks glass combine all the ingredients except the Scotch. Add a 2 inch ice cube and stir for 5 seconds. Using an atomizer top the double rocks glass with six sprays of Scotch.

cocktails.name: Shuttlecock
cocktails.garnish: blackberries 3 for muddling and 1, Orange half wheel for muddling plus orange wheel, Mint plouche
cocktails.instructions: In a cocktail tin muddle the mint leaves three of the blackberries and the orange half wheel then add the remaining ingredients except the club soda and garnishes. Fill a Pilsner glass with crushed ice. Add three 1¼ inch ice cubes to the cocktail tin. Cover and whip. Strain the cocktail into the Pilsner glass and add the club soda. Skewer the remaining orange wheel and blackberry to create a flag then garnish with the mint plouche and the orange blackberry flag.

cocktails.name: Sippy Cup
cocktails.instructions: In a cocktail tin combine the ginger syrup lime juice vermouth and amaro. Fill a highball glass with 1¼ inch ice cubes. Fill the cocktail tin with ice. Cover and shake vigorously. Strain the cocktail into the highball glass. Add the Angostura bitters to the top of the cocktail and top with the club soda. Skewer the orange half wheel through the ends and place the mint sprigs through the center then garnish the cocktail with the orange mint flag.

cocktails.name: Summer of Yes
cocktails.instructions: In a cocktail tin muddle the cucumber slice then add the remaining ingredients except the beer and garnish. Wrap the rhubarb garnish around the inside of a highball glass starting from the bottom. Fill the glass with 1¼ inch ice cubes and add the beer. Add 1¼ inch ice cubes to the cocktail tin. Cover and shake vigorously. Strain the cocktail into the highball glass.

cocktails.name: Superbas
cocktails.instructions: Combine all the ingredients except the beer and garnish in a cocktail tin. Fill a highball glass with 1¼ inch ice cubes and add the beer. Fill the tin with ice. Cover and shake vigorously. Strain the cocktail into the highball glass and express the oils of the grapefruit twist over the glass. Rub the twist on the rim of the glass and drop it into the glass leaving the flesh side up.

cocktails.name: Velvet Cobbler
cocktails.instructions: Combine all the ingredients except the garnish in a cocktail tin. Fill a cobbler glass with crushed ice. Add three 1¼ inch ice cubes to the tin. Cover and whip. Strain the cocktail into the cobbler glass and garnish with the lemon wheel.
cocktails.notes: Creator: Leo Robitschek and Eamon Rockey

cocktails.name: All Betz Are Off
cocktails.instructions: Combine all the ingredients except the garnish in a mixing glass. Place a 2 inch ice cube in a double rocks glass. Fill the mixing glass with ice and stir. Strain the cocktail into the double rocks glass and express the oils of the lemon twist over the glass. Rub the twist on the rim of the glass and drop it into the glass leaving the flesh side up.

cocktails.name: Amelia
cocktails.instructions: In a cocktail tin combine all the ingredients except the mezcal. Seal and dry shake to emulsify the egg white into the cocktail. Carefully open the tin and fill with 1¼ inch ice cubes. Seal it again and shake vigorously. Strain the cocktail into a chilled fizz glass using a hawthorne strainer and a fine tea strainer. Using an atomizer top with six sprays of mezcal.

cocktails.name: Argyle
cocktails.instructions: Combine all the ingredients except the garnish in a mixing glass. Fill the glass with ice and stir. Strain the cocktail into a coupe and express the oils of the lemon twist over the glass. Rub the twist on the rim of the glass and drop it into the glass leaving the flesh side up.

cocktails.name: Avo
cocktails.instructions: In a cocktail tin combine all the ingredients except the garnishes. Fill a Pilsner glass with crushed ice. Add three 1¼ inch ice cubes to the tin. Cover and whip. Strain the cocktail into the Pilsner glass and top with additional crushed ice. Garnish with cacao powder and the mint plouche.

cocktails.name: Beekeeper
cocktails.instructions: Combine all the ingredients except the egg white in a cocktail tin. Add the egg white to the tin cover and dry shake to emulsify the egg white into the cocktail. Fill the tin with ice cover again and shake vigorously. Strain the cocktail into the coupe.

cocktails.name: Berry Dangerous Fix
cocktails.instructions: In a cocktail tin muddle a strawberry then add the remaining ingredients except the garnish. Fill a double rocks glass with crushed ice. Add three 1¼ inch ice cubes to the cocktail tin. Cover and whip. Strain the cocktail into the double rocks glass. Fill the glass with more crushed ice and make a mound of crushed ice up over the rim of the glass. Garnish with the remaining strawberry.

cocktails.name: Black Dahlia
cocktails.instructions: Combine all the ingredients except the garnish in a mixing glass. Fill the glass with ice and stir. Strain the cocktail into a Nick and Nora glass and express the oils of the grapefruit twist over the glass. Rub the twist on the rim of the glass and drop it into the glass leaving the flesh side up.

cocktails.name: Block Party
cocktails.instructions: In a cocktail tin combine all the ingredients except the garnishes. Add the lime wheels to a Pilsner glass going up from the base in a clockwise motion and then fill the glass with crushed ice. Add three 1¼ inch ice cubes to the cocktail tin. Cover and whip. Strain the cocktail into the Pilsner glass and top with additional crushed ice. Garnish with the mint plouche.

cocktails.name: Bohemia
cocktails.instructions: Combine all the ingredients except the lemon twist in a mixing glass. Fill the glass with ice and stir. Strain the cocktail into a coupe and express the oils of the lemon twist over the glass. Rub the twist on the rim of the glass and drop it into the glass leaving the flesh side up.

cocktails.name: Bread and Butternut
cocktails.instructions: In a cocktail tin combine all the ingredients except the garnish. Fill a Pilsner glass with crushed ice. Add three 1¼ inch ice cubes to the cocktail tin. Cover and whip. Strain the cocktail into the Pilsner glass and top with additional crushed ice. Garnish with the lemon wheel.

cocktails.name: Chi Chi Swizzle

ingredients.name: Red Bell Pepper and Thai Bird Chile-Infused Barolo Chinato

cocktails.instructions: Combine all the ingredients except the lemon twist in a cocktail tin. Fill a Pilsner glass with crushed ice. Add three 1¼ inch ice cubes to the tin. Cover and whip. Strain the cocktail into the Pilsner glass twist the horse's neck lemon twist into a tight coil and lay it on top of the glass.

cocktails.name: Choked Up
cocktails.garnish: kumquat cut in half ¾ for muddling and ¾, cucumber slices 1 for muddling and 1, Mint plouche
cocktails.instructions: Use the lemon wedge to moisten the top inch of the outer rim of a chilled double rocks glass. Gently roll the double rocks glass in salt to rim half of the glass. Shake off excess salt. Use the corner of a folded cocktail napkin to make the salt a consistent thickness. In a cocktail tin muddle a kumquat half a cucumber slice and the mint leaves then add the remaining ingredients except the garnishes. Fill the double rocks glass with 1¼ inch ice cubes. Fill the cocktail tin with 1¼ inch ice cubes. Cover and shake vigorously. Strain the cocktail into the double rocks glass. Garnish with the mint plouche and remaining cucumber slice and kumquat half.

cocktails.name: Coq Tail 1
cocktails.instructions: In a cocktail tin combine all the ingredients. Fill a rooster cup with crushed ice. Add three 1¼ inch ice cubes to the cocktail tin. Cover and whip vigorously. Strain the cocktail into the rooster cup using a hawthorne strainer and top with additional crushed ice.

cocktails.name: Coq Tail 2
cocktails.instructions: In a cocktail tin combine all the ingredients. Fill a rooster cup with crushed ice. Add three 1¼ inch ice cubes to the cocktail tin. Cover and whip vigorously. Strain the cocktail into the rooster cup using a hawthorne strainer and top with additional crushed ice.
cocktails.notes: Creator: Nathan O'Neill Pietro Collina and Leo Robitschek

cocktails.name: Coq Tail 3
cocktails.instructions: In a cocktail tin combine all the ingredients. Fill a rooster cup with crushed ice. Add three 1¼ inch ice cubes to the cocktail tin. Cover and whip vigorously. Strain the cocktail into the rooster cup using a hawthorne strainer and top with additional crushed ice.
cocktails.notes: Creator: Nathan O'Neill Pietro Collina and Leo Robitschek

cocktails.name: Deli Slang
cocktails.instructions: In a cocktail tin combine all the ingredients except the garnish. Seal and dry shake to emulsify the egg white into the cocktail. Carefully open the tin and fill with 1¼ inch ice cubes. Seal it again and shake vigorously. Strain the cocktail into a chilled coupe using a hawthorne strainer and a fine tea strainer. Once the egg white settles use a Japanese bitters dasher to carefully add dots of horseradish tincture around the rim of the glass.

cocktails.name: Dewin' It

cocktails.name: Madison Park Smash
cocktails.instructions: Combine the demerara syrup lemon juice Royal Combier and Cognac in a cocktail tin. Clap the mint leaves between your hands to release their oils then drop them in the tin. Fill a double rocks glass with crushed ice. Add three 1¼ inch ice cubes to the tin cover and gently whip to chill and aerate the cocktail without overly bruising the mint leaves. Strain the cocktail into the double rocks glass. Fill the glass with more crushed ice and make a mound of crushed ice up over the rim of the glass. Insert straws and the mint plouche beside the mound just inside the glass. Decorate the ice dome with the Angostura bitters.

cocktails.name: Manhattan Milk Punch
cocktails.instructions: In a small saucepan warm the milk over medium heat stirring continuously until it starts steaming. Make sure that it doesn't boil or burn. Remove from the heat and add the lemon juice. Give the mixture a quick stir it should start curdling. In a large container combine the curdled milk with all the remaining ingredients. Hang a superbag over an empty container. If you don't have a superbag get a large toddy filter and hang it over the large container or cover a chinois with a few layers of cheesecloth. Pour the liquid into the superbag when the liquid that is coming out starts running clear you should have a layer of milk curds in the superbag. Make sure you do not break this layer as it's the clarification layer of the punch. Allow the remaining liquid to drip through and clarify. For each serving place a 2 inch ice cube in a double rocks glass and pour in about 4 ounces of the milk punch.

Strain the cocktail into the highball glass. Top with additional crushed ice to reach the rim of the glass. Insert the lemon wheel along the back of the glass.
